name: Acadian Eight Bean Chili Annie Rosensweigs'
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb Each of the following beans
1/4 cup Coriander seeds, toasted and ground
1/4 cup Cayenne pepper or to taste (for the timid tongue)
1/2 cup Dried Poblano Chili peppers, ground
1 can 108 oz Italian tomatoes with juice
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over and wash the beans.
2. Place in a large pot and cover with 4 quarts cold water. Soak overnight.
3. Wash and drain the beans. Cover with water, bring to a boil, lower heat, and simmer for 2 hours or until tender.
4. Cook bacon in a large skillet, drain, and crumble.
5. Add the remaining ingredients (garlic, coriander, cinnamon, paprika, cayenne pepper, dried Poblano chili peppers) to the skillet and sauté for 5 minutes.
6. Add tomatoes and beer; simmer.
7. In another skillet, sauté ground beef until no longer pink.
8. When beans are tender, drain them, reserving the liquid.
9. Add meat, bacon, and vegetables to the beans.
10. Simmer over low heat until hot, adding bean liquid if necessary.
